bullets.text: Q3 companywide CR 96.6 (YTD through September exactly 96.0 on target); Personal Lines Q3 CR 96.2 with nearly 3 CR points of catastrophe losses primarily from Louisiana floods; Commercial Lines CR 99.3 in Q3 (YTD 94.5) as frequency spike drove rate/underwriting actions.

NPW grew 12% in Q3; net earned premium grew 13%; Commercial Lines Q3 NPW grew 21% and net earned premium grew 23%; total Personal Lines remained solid despite advertising pullback designed to protect profit goal.

Robinson (auto+home bundle) policies added in Q3 nearly 50% more than Q3 2015; Robinson year-over-year retention improvement exceeded 10% — a breakthrough; Agency auto PIFs growing; catastrophe team closed 95%+ of Louisiana flood claims rapidly.

Issued $500M 10-year senior notes at 2.45% (92 bps over 10-year Treasury) during Q3; Q3 FTE investment return 1.3%; capital maintained to support growth within 30% debt-to-total capital policy; strong underwriting capital generation YTD.

Louisiana flood catastrophe a single-event claim record for that peril at Progressive; Hurricane Matthew struck Florida and Carolinas in early October causing ~$85M losses; catastrophe team deployed from Louisiana directly to Matthew response closing ~60% of claims by end of October.

Commercial Lines rate and underwriting actions taken post-haste after frequency spike recognition; rate increases on annual policies will take time to earn in; company committed to monitoring closely and maintaining profitability priority over growth.

Commercial Lines CR elevated by frequency spike but management confident actions addressing root cause; no systemic loss reserve miss identified; fast-growing Commercial Lines organization at record scale presenting management challenge.

Agency and Direct auto showing solid new policy application growth in Q3; Robinson growth in both channels with Platinum agency partners doubling in ranks; Progressive Advantage Agency expanding to serve bundled customers online and by phone.

First shareholder letter written by Tricia Griffith as CEO (Tricia took over from Glenn Renwick in Q2 2016); Tricia highlighted Destination Era strategy continuity and strong leadership team in place; entire executive team engaged in Investor Relations meeting Destination Era progress update.

Progressive intentionally hired and trained ahead of need to serve customers during catastrophic events; catastrophe volume in 2016 on pace to be record year; claims response to Louisiana and Matthew events showcased culture and retention-building customer care capability.
